title: Chicken Chettinad

overview.description: A fragrant South Indian chicken curry made with freshly roasted spices, curry leaves, coconut and black pepper. Richly aromatic without relying on heavy cream.

ingredients: 800g bone-in chicken thighs and drumsticks (or boneless thighs)
method: Dry fry all the masala ingredients except the coconut over a medium heat until fragrant.
Add the coconut and toast for 1 minute.
Allow to cool, then grind to a fine powder.
Heat the ghee over a medium heat.
Cook the onions for 10–12 minutes until deeply golden.
Add the garlic, ginger and curry leaves.
Stir in the ground Chettinad masala.
Cook until the tomatoes have broken down and the oil begins to separate.
Add the chicken and coat well in the masala.
Pour in the coconut milk and 150ml water.
Cover and cook for 35–40 minutes until the chicken is tender.
Scatter with fresh coriander before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Chettinad cuisine from Tamil Nadu is famous throughout India for its complex spice blends and generous use of black pepper and curry leaves.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to toast and grind whole spices, build a South Indian curry base and use coconut to balance bold spice flavours.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Introduces the distinctive flavours of South Indian cooking and showcases how different regional Indian curries can be.
chapman-tips: Freshly toasted spices are the heart of this dish—don't substitute pre-ground spices for the whole masala.
Frozen curry leaves work almost as well as fresh and are worth keeping in the freezer.
Bone-in chicken produces the richest flavour, but boneless thighs make an excellent everyday alternative.
Allow the onions to become properly golden before adding the spices.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions once cooled.
notes.leftovers: The curry develops an even deeper flavour overnight and reheats beautifully.
